response: Charles A. Agemian and Arshile Gorky both had significant career changes, but the nature and extent of their changes differ. Charles A. Agemian retired from Chase Manhattan Bank and later became chairman and CEO of Garden State National Bank, indicating a major career shift. Arshile Gorky, on the other hand, was a prominent figure in the abstract expressionist movement, known for his influential works and contributions to art, but he did not experience the same level of career change as Agemian. Gorky's career was more focused on his artistic achievements and his role in the movement, with fewer major transitions in his professional life.
